brief:
TOPIC: MRI-based personalized heart digital twins to noninvasively predict reentrant VT circuits and optimal ablation lesion sites in scar-dependent ventricular tachycardia.

GAP / WHY IT MATTERS:
- Catheter ablation of scar-dependent VT limited by hemodynamic instability, long procedure times, high recurrence.
- Need for noninvasive prediction of VT circuits and best ablation targets before/during the procedure.
- Unknown how well digital twin-predicted VTs and ablation target sets correspond to invasively mapped clinical counterparts.

DESIGN / SETTING:
- Combined clinical + computational (digital twin) study.
- Each patient's digital twin built from preprocedural contrast-enhanced cardiac MRI.
- VT simulated in models using rapid pacing protocols; ablation targets derived that would terminate all possible simulated VTs.
- Patients then underwent invasive VT ablation including targeting of diastolic activity and optimum entrainment sites.
- Digital twin-predicted VT circuits and lesions compared head-to-head with invasive clinical findings.

POPULATION / N:
- 18 patients with scar-dependent VT.

INTERVENTION / COMPARISON:
- Comparison: digital twin model predictions (VT circuits, critical sites, ablation target areas) vs invasive clinical mapping/ablation results.

KEY RESULTS (exact numbers):
- 43 clinical VTs induced; 92 digital twin VTs simulated.
- Diastolic activity present in 16 of 43 clinical VTs (37.2%).
- Digital twin detection of critical VT sites: sensitivity 81.3%, specificity 83.8%, PPV 21.7%, NPV 98.8%.
- Agreement at AHA-segment level between clinical VT critical sites and digital twin primary predicted sites: moderate, kappa = 0.46 (+/- 0.32; P<=0.001).
- VT termination by ablation at a digital twin-predicted site: 4 of 5 (80%) cases where attempted.
- 426 of 709 (60.1%) lesions were within 5 mm of a predicted target site.
- 54.0% (+/- 28.9%) of the digital twin-predicted area was ablated per patient using conventional mapping criteria.

MAIN TAKEAWAY:
- Heart digital twin VT circuits and ablation targets accurately predict many features of clinical counterparts, but have limitations in spatial resolution.
- High NPV / sensitivity but low PPV; moderate spatial agreement.
- Demonstrates significant potential of digital twin technology to guide catheter ablation for scar-dependent VT.
